title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Which Deaths Are Not Covered by Term Insurance Policy?
Meta description: A term insurance plan doesn’t cover all kinds of deaths. If the death occurs under certain specific circumstances, the insurer may reject the death claim.
Keywords: term insurance cover, term cover, online term plan, term plan premium calculator
A term insurance policy is one of the most cost-effective ways through which you can secure your family’s future. It provides some much-needed financial assistance to the nominees of a policyholder upon the said policyholder’s death. The payout that the nominees receive can be used by them to further their life goals as they see fit.
That said, not all deaths are covered by a term insurance plan. As a matter of fact, there are certain situations where an insurance service provider might end up rejecting the death claim filed by the nominees. Wondering what kinds of death are out of the purview of a term insurance cover? Continue reading to find out. But before we go ahead, let’s quickly take a look at the concept of death claim.
What is a Death Claim?
In the event of the policyholder’s death during the policy term, the nominees of the policyholder are required to make a formal request to the insurance company for the disbursal of death benefit as applicable under the plan. This request that the nominees make is termed as a death claim.
However, before disbursing the death benefit payout, the insurance company usually verifies the veracity of the death claim. And during this process of verification, if the insurer finds any discrepancies or mistakes, the death claim may be rejected and the payout willnot be made to the nominees.
Kinds of Death That Are Not Covered by a Term Insurance Policy
The death claim may be rejected by the insurance service provider if the policyholder’s death is not covered under the term insurance plan. Here’s a quick overview of the kinds of death that a term cover doesn’t provide for.
Death by suicide
Conclusion
There you have it. The different kinds of death that are not covered by a typical term insurance policy. That said, before you go ahead and purchase a term insurance plan, make sure to read through the exclusions to get a better idea of what’s not covered. Also, make sure to use a term plan premium calculator to determine the amount of premium that you will have to pay towards the plan.

word_comments.author: Sangeetha V/Pune HO/Product Development/Life
word_comments.comment: Do we mean by fraud here? If so mention that. Discrepancy and mistake cannot be reason for death claim to be rejected. Suggest getting this line checked from claims team also
